description_long: This object is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a predominantly white fuselage and a blue vertical stabilizer at the tail. The wings are mounted high on the fuselage and extend straight out, with brown sections that likely represent access panels or walkways. The nose features a black propeller and a cockpit area with dark-tinted windows, suggesting a single or dual-seat configuration. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller nose wheel. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The overall design is reminiscent of a light general aviation aircraft, suitable for short-range flights and urban airfields.
description: A small, white high-wing airplane with a blue tail, fixed landing gear, and a black propeller at the nose. The cockpit is forward-mounted with dark windows, and the wings are straight with brown panel details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wings, brown panel details, black propeller, and blue tail fin.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose-mounted propeller, forward cockpit, and the full wingspan with brown panels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the high-wing configuration, fixed landing gear, and blue tail section.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the blue vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the tail section's structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with dark windows, likely single or dual-seat.
